mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive growth in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer demand and confidence, which directly translates to higher corporate revenues and earnings, thereby driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The 20-day forward S&P 500 returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS growth over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a critical threshold signals a tightening labor market, which typically precedes wage inflation and strong consumer spending, boosting corporate profits and stock prices over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:historical correlation of low unemployment with economic expansion
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of instances when UNRATE is below 4.5% for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of accelerating inflation (CPIAUCSL) and an increasing federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals an aggressive monetary tightening stance by the central bank. This leads to higher discount rates and reduced corporate profitability expectations, negatively impacting short-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 3% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change > 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:central bank reaction function to inflation
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC is below 0 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong industrial production growth (INDPRO), companies are expanding output, indicating healthy economic activity and robust demand for goods. This translates to higher earnings and positive equity market sentiment over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of periods where INDPRO growth exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) indicates a weakening in the construction sector and broader economic demand. This often signals an impending economic slowdown, leading to reduced corporate earnings and negatively impacting longer-term equity returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month average falls below 1500 (thousands of units) and remains there for 2 consecutive months
evidence_refs: fact:housing starts as a leading economic indicator
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive or flat in more than 50% of instances despite sustained low housing starts.

mechanism_narrative: Robust non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) growth signifies a healthy and expanding job market, which underpins strong consumer confidence and spending. This directly supports corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods when PAYEMS growth is strong.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained period of high federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a restrictive monetary policy stance. This increases borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, eventually dampening economic activity and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:impact of high interest rates on economic activity
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns remain positive or flat in more than 45% of instances during periods of sustained high FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: When strong GDP growth (GDPC1) is accompanied by rising inflation (CPIAUCSL), it signals an overheating economy. This condition often prompts aggressive monetary tightening, leading to short-term market volatility and negative returns as investors price in higher rates and potential economic slowdown.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 2-quarter annualized growth > 4% AND CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:historical central bank response to overheating economy
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns are positive or flat in more than 40% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC is below 0 over any 1-year rolling window.
